aurora.neighborhoods: Aurora Highlands, Parker Road Corridor, City Center (A-Town), Del Mar, Heather Gardens, Meadow Hills
aurora.meetingHighlights: Growing suburban meetings with many community and church-based groups; active newcomer and study meetings around A-Town and Parker Road.
aurora.transitInfo: RTD light rail and bus connections link Aurora with Denver; Denver International Airport (DEN) is nearby for regional and international flights.

colorado-springs.neighborhoods: Old Colorado City, Broadmoor, Downtown, Ivywild, North End, Briargate
colorado-springs.meetingHighlights: Active mountain-region fellowship with strong downtown and Broadmoor area meetings; many outdoor and recovery events tied to the Pikes Peak community.
colorado-springs.transitInfo: Mountain Metropolitan Transit (MMT) provides city bus service; Colorado Springs Airport (COS) serves regional routes.

boulder.areaDescription: Boulder area at the base of the Rocky Mountains
boulder.neighborhoods: Pearl Street, University Hill, North Boulder, South Boulder, Chautauqua, Gunbarrel
boulder.meetingHighlights: Progressive college-town fellowship with active meetings near CU Boulder campus; strong focus on young people's meetings and outdoor recovery events in the Flatirons.
boulder.transitInfo: RTD bus service connects Boulder to Denver; Regional Transportation District (RTD) routes serve Boulder County.

denver.neighborhoods: LoDo, River North (RiNo), Capitol Hill, Uptown, Park Hill, Cherry Creek
denver.meetingHighlights: Large and diverse AA community with meetings across neighborhoods including downtown LoDo and RiNo; many specialty meetings and strong newcomer resources.
denver.transitInfo: RTD buses and light rail provide extensive coverage; Denver International Airport (DEN) is the major regional hub.

pueblo.neighborhoods: Downtown, Bessemer, Mesa Junction, Union Avenue
pueblo.meetingHighlights: Small-city meetings with solid neighborhood attendance; many groups meet in downtown churches and community centers.
pueblo.transitInfo: Pueblo Transit serves the city; Pueblo Memorial Airport (PUB) and regional highways provide access.

fort-collins.areaDescription: Fort Collins and northern Colorado along the Cache la Poudre River
fort-collins.neighborhoods: Old Town, Campus West near CSU, Midtown, South Fort Collins, Timnath, Wellington
fort-collins.meetingHighlights: Thriving college-town recovery community near Colorado State University; active Old Town and Campus West meetings; strong young people's groups and outdoor recovery fellowship in the Poudre Canyon area.
fort-collins.transitInfo: Transfort bus system serves Fort Collins; FLEX regional bus connects to Loveland and Longmont; Northern Colorado Regional Airport (FNL); Interstate 25 access.

lakewood.areaDescription: Lakewood and the western Denver metro foothills
lakewood.neighborhoods: Belmar, Green Mountain, Union Boulevard corridor, Bear Creek, Applewood, West Colfax
lakewood.meetingHighlights: Active West Denver metro meetings with groups near Belmar and along Union Boulevard; gateway to mountain recovery events; mix of Big Book study, discussion, and speaker formats.
lakewood.transitInfo: RTD light rail (W Line) and bus routes connect Lakewood to downtown Denver; close to US-6 and C-470; Denver International Airport (DEN) accessible via light rail.

thornton.areaDescription: North metro Denver city in Adams and Weld counties
thornton.neighborhoods: Original Thornton, Northglenn border area, Eastlake, Thornton Town Center, Hunters Glen, Trail Winds area
thornton.meetingHighlights: North metro meetings serve Thornton and nearby suburbs with regular neighborhood-based discussion groups.
thornton.transitInfo: RTD buses and N Line commuter rail connect Thornton with downtown Denver; I-25 is the main corridor.

greeley.areaDescription: Weld County regional center on the northern Front Range
greeley.neighborhoods: Downtown, UNC area, West Greeley, Evans vicinity, Bittersweet, Promontory
greeley.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve the university community and the wider Weld County region with dependable weekly schedules.
greeley.transitInfo: Greeley-Evans Transit and Bustang connections support travel; US-34 and US-85 provide regional access.

grand-junction.areaDescription: Western Colorado hub in the Grand Valley
grand-junction.neighborhoods: Downtown, Redlands, Orchard Mesa, North Avenue corridor, Fruitvale, Horizon Drive area
grand-junction.meetingHighlights: Regional meetings draw from Mesa County and nearby mountain communities with regular in-town options.
grand-junction.transitInfo: Grand Valley Transit serves the city; Grand Junction Regional Airport and I-70 provide broader access.
